summary: The task was identifying a specific muscle whose origin does not involve seeding to the greater tubercle of the humerus among given choices; after reviewing multiple anatomical resources, Subscapularis stands out as inserting at the lesser rather than the greater tubercle. Thus, no other listed option besides Supraspinatus meets all criteria for being connected via insertion specifically to the mentioned area. Agent concluded by reaffirming their answer based on consistent evidence from medical literature regarding musculature attachments around shoulder anatomy structures like those specified here
